question: How many programs does FE-RUPP offer in total?
ground_truth_answer: FE-RUPP offers 9 programs in total: 4 Honor Bachelor programs, 3 Bachelor programs, and 2 Master programs.

question: What are the Honor Bachelor programs offered at FE-RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The 4 Honor Bachelor programs are: Food Technology And Engineering, Data Science And Engineering, Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering, and Environmental Engineering.

question: What Bachelor programs are available at FE-RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The 3 Bachelor programs are: Bio Engineering Biotechnology, Information Technology Engineering, and Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering.

question: What Master programs does FE-RUPP offer?
ground_truth_answer: FE-RUPP offers 2 Master programs: Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program (3 years) and Master of Science in Information Technology Engineering - MITE (2 years).

question: Which program at FE-RUPP has a partnership with Osaka Prefecture University?
ground_truth_answer: The Food Technology And Engineering (Honor Bachelor) program was developed with participation of experts and researchers from Osaka Prefecture University.

question: What degree does the Data Science And Engineering program award?
ground_truth_answer: The Data Science And Engineering program awards a Bachelor (Honors) degree with 4 years duration taught in English.

question: When was the Faculty of Engineering at RUPP established?
ground_truth_answer: The Faculty of Engineering at Royal University of Phnom Penh was established on May 07, 2013.

question: What subjects are in Foundation Year Semester 1 of Food Technology And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year Semester 1 of Food Technology includes: English Composition (Critical thinking, reading and writing), General Mathematics (Algebra), General Science 1 (General Chemistry), General Physics, Fundamental Computer and Programming, Sociology (Digital skill and Problem Solving), Personal Development and Management, Introduction to Engineering.

question: What courses are studied in Year 2 of Food Technology And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of Food Technology includes courses such as: Introduction to Food Technology, Principle of Analytical Chemistry, Principle of Physical Chemistry, Organic Chemistry, Introduction to Microbiology, Seminar in Food Technology, Food Processing 1, Principle of Instrumental Analysis, Biochemistry, Food Chemistry, Food Processing, Food Properties, and Food Microbiology.

question: What subjects are in Year 3 of Food Technology And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Food Technology includes: Chemistry and Physical for Food Analysis, Food Nutrition, Design and Experimental Planning Food Science, Food Product Development, and other advanced food science and engineering courses.

question: What courses are in Year 4 of Food Technology And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of Food Technology includes: Food Toxicology and Additive, Technology in Carbohydrate and Protein, Final Project (Industry Training), and Thesis (or Extended Training/Coursework as optional tracks).

question: What are the final semester track options in Food Technology And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Food Technology offers 3 final semester track options: (1) Coursework - for students who want to conduct coursework and final project; (2) Research Thesis - for outstanding students who want to conduct research under faculty supervision; (3) Extended Training - for students who want to participate in a long cooperative training program with companies.

question: What subjects are studied in Year 2 of Data Science And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of Data Science And Engineering includes: Math III (Calculus II), Probability and Statistics, Discrete Math, Statistics, and other computing and data foundation courses.

question: What are the Foundation Year courses for Data Science And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for Data Science includes: English Composition, Mathematics I (Algebra), Technical and Business Writing, Mathematics II (Calculus I), and other foundational courses.

question: What courses are in Year 3 of Data Science And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Data Science includes: Introduction to Big Data and Web Mining, Artificial Intelligence, Optimization and Process Analytics, Cognitive Computing, and other advanced data science courses.

question: What subjects are in Year 4 of Data Science And Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of Data Science includes: Professional Computing and Ethics, Marketing Analytics, Leadership Skill, and Extended Training (full semester cooperative program with a company).

question: Does Data Science And Engineering require an internship?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, Data Science And Engineering students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

question: What courses are in Year 2 of Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of Automation Engineering includes: Introduction of Algorithms and Object-oriented Programming, Introduction to Management Science, Database Systems and Applications, Organizational Behavior, and other computing and management courses.

question: What Foundation Year subjects does Automation And Supply Chain Engineering have?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for Automation Engineering includes: Introduction to Computers and Programming, General Science I, Digital Skill and Problem Solving, Introduction to Supply Chain Management, and other foundational courses.

question: What subjects are in Year 3 of Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Automation Engineering includes: Mobile App Development, Procurement and Supply Chain Management, Business Intelligence and Analytics, Inventory and Production Management, and other advanced courses.

question: What is the maximum allowed study duration for Automation And Supply Chain Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: The maximum study duration for Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering is 5 years (normal duration is 4 years).

question: What courses are in Year 2 of Environmental Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of Environmental Engineering includes: English for Academic 3 and 4, Analytical Chemistry, Earth Environmental Science Statistics, and other environmental science foundational courses.

question: What Foundation Year subjects does Environmental Engineering have?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for Environmental Engineering includes: English for Academic 1 and 2, General Mathematics 1 and 2, and other preparatory courses.

question: What courses are in Year 3 of Environmental Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Environmental Engineering includes: English for Academic 5 and 6, Hydraulics, Computer Applications and Software in Environmental Engineering, and other advanced environmental courses.

question: What courses are in Year 4 of Environmental Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of Environmental Engineering includes: Basic Environmental Toxicology, Building Sanitation, and Comprehensive Environmental Engineering Design, among other capstone courses.

question: What subjects are in Year 3 of Bio Engineering Biotechnology?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Bio Engineering Biotechnology includes: Biochemistry II, Unit Operation for Food Engineer II, and other advanced biotechnology and engineering courses.

question: What courses are in Year 4 of Bio Engineering Biotechnology?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of Bio Engineering Biotechnology includes: Food Technology II, Immunology, Ethics in Biotechnology, and Research Thesis or Engineering Training.

question: What Foundation Year courses are in Bio Engineering Biotechnology?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for Bio Engineering Biotechnology includes: English, Mathematics I, Mathematics II, and other foundational science and engineering courses.

question: Does Bio Engineering Biotechnology require an internship?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, Bio Engineering Biotechnology students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during vacation time in the third year or before.

question: What courses are in Year 2 Semester 1 of Information Technology Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 Semester 1 of IT Engineering includes: Advanced Programming Language I and Data Structure and Algorithm I, among other computing courses.

question: What subjects are in Year 3 of Information Technology Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of IT Engineering includes: Network Engineering I and II, Web Technologies and Cloud Computing I and II, and other advanced IT infrastructure courses.

question: What courses are in Year 4 of Information Technology Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of IT Engineering includes: Mobile Application Development II, Management Information System, Fin Tech, and Internship (Hard Skill) & Thesis.

question: What Foundation Year subjects does Information Technology Engineering have?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for IT Engineering includes: Mathematics I, Computer Fundamental, Mathematics II, Technical Drawing, and other preparatory courses.

question: What is the minimum GPA required to write a thesis in Information Technology Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Students in Information Technology Engineering need a minimum GPA of 3.5 to be eligible to write a thesis.

question: What courses are in Year 2 of Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of Telecommunication Engineering includes: Mathematics III, Circuit Theory I, Advanced Mathematics, Circuit Theory II, and other electronics and mathematics courses.

question: What subjects are studied in Year 3 of Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 3 of Telecommunication Engineering includes: Communication Engineering, Electronic Engineering I, PLC for Automation System, and Electronic Engineering II & IoT. Some courses marked with * are part of ASEAN FACTORI 4.0 project.

question: What Foundation Year subjects does Telecommunication Engineering include?
ground_truth_answer: Foundation Year for Telecommunication Engineering includes: Mathematics I, Introduction to Electronics Engineering, Mathematics II, Introduction to Electrical Engineering, and other preparatory courses.

question: What courses are in Year 4 of Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Year 4 of Telecommunication Engineering includes: Business Management and Entrepreneurship, Control Engineering, Engineering in Startup, and Thesis.

question: What is special about some courses in Telecommunication Engineering curriculum?
ground_truth_answer: Some courses in the Telecommunication Engineering curriculum are marked with * and are under Project ASEAN FACTORI 4.0 (609854-EPP-1-2019-1-FR-EPPKA2 CBHE-JP).

question: What courses are in Year 1 of the Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program?
ground_truth_answer: Year 1 of the Biotechnology Master includes: Instrumental Analysis, Applied and Multivariate Statistics, Bioinformatics, Techniques in Biotechnology, and other advanced biotechnology courses.

question: What courses are in Year 2 of the Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of the Biotechnology Master includes: Fermentation Technology, Plant Biochemistry / Molecular Biology, and Thesis Work.

question: How long is the Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program and how is it structured?
ground_truth_answer: The Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program is 3 years long, covering advanced topics in biotechnology, food science, bioengineering, and sustainable processing of biological raw materials, culminating in a thesis.

question: What courses are in Year 1 of the MITE (Master of Science in Information Technology Engineering) program?
ground_truth_answer: Year 1 of MITE includes: Advanced Network Computing, Software Engineering, Database Design and Application, Computer Graphics, and other advanced IT engineering courses.

question: What courses are in Year 2 of the MITE master program?
ground_truth_answer: Year 2 of MITE includes: Mobile Application, Machine Learning & Natural Language Processing, Multimedia, General ICT4D, and other advanced specialization courses.

question: What are the specialization tracks available in the MITE master program?
ground_truth_answer: The MITE master program offers 3 specialization tracks: (1) Software Development Area, (2) Network Engineering Area, and (3) Multimedia Science Area.

question: What are the research areas of the Data Science And Engineering program?
ground_truth_answer: Data Science And Engineering focuses on 4 research areas: Web and Cloud Technology, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Big Data, and Business Intelligence.

question: What research areas does Bio Engineering Biotechnology focus on?
ground_truth_answer: Bio Engineering Biotechnology covers 3 research areas: Biological Molecular Medicine, Agro-Industrial Biotechnology, and Biotechnology to Create Biological Devices.

question: What are the research focus areas of Environmental Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Environmental Engineering focuses on 4 research areas: Air & Climate, Water, Soil & Ecosystems, and Energy & Resources.

question: What research areas does Information Technology Engineering cover?
ground_truth_answer: Information Technology Engineering covers research in: Artificial Intelligence, Data Mining, Image Processing and Analysis, and Computer Vision.

question: What are the research areas in Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: Telecommunication Engineering research covers: Wireless Communication Systems, Electronics and Electromagnetic Signal Systems, Telephone Communication System, and Satellite System.

question: What research focus does the Biotechnology and Food Technology Master program have?
ground_truth_answer: The Biotechnology Master program focuses on: Biotechnology, Bioengineering, Food Science, and Sustainable Processing of Biological Raw Materials.

question: What international cooperation does the MITE master program have?
ground_truth_answer: MITE has cooperation with international universities in Japan, Europe and USA, and is a member of the ASEAN University Network with MOUs with partner universities including Group T Belgium, Waseda University Japan, Dresden University of Technology Germany, and IWR Heidelberg University Germany.

question: What teaching methodology or framework does FE-RUPP use?
ground_truth_answer: FE-RUPP uses the CDIO (Conceive, Design, Implement, Operate) framework to enhance engineering education and build programs toward international level.

question: Which program has a partnership with SIIT Thammasat University Thailand?
ground_truth_answer: Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering has a partnership with Sirindhorn International Institute of Technology (SIIT), Thammasat University (TU), Thailand, including a 2+2 transfer program option.

question: What are the career prospects for Food Technology And Engineering graduates?
ground_truth_answer: Food Technology graduates can work as: Supervisor/Production Planner, QA and QC Specialist/Food Safety Specialist/Laboratory Manager, Food Technologist/R&D Chef, and Business Analyzer/Startup founder.

question: What jobs can Data Science And Engineering graduates get?
ground_truth_answer: Data Science graduates can work as: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Associate Data Engineer, and Data Engineer, among other data-related roles.

question: What career options are available for Bio Engineering Biotechnology graduates?
ground_truth_answer: Bio Engineering graduates can work as: Agro-industry & Food Specialist, Biomolecular Engineer, Biological Materials Engineer, Professor or Researcher of Biotechnology, and Research or Biological Laboratory Specialist.

question: What are the job prospects for Environmental Engineering graduates?
ground_truth_answer: Environmental Engineering graduates can work as: Environmental Specialist, Climate Change Specialist, Water/Wastewater Engineer, and Waste Management Specialist.

question: What careers can Information Technology Engineering graduates pursue?
ground_truth_answer: IT Engineering graduates can work as: Agile Software Developer, Agile Web Developer, Agile Mobile Developer, Fullstack Developer, Network & Security Engineer, and Data Engineer.

question: What are the career prospects for Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering graduates?
ground_truth_answer: Telecommunication Engineering graduates can work as: Electrical Engineer, Telecommunication Engineer, Broadcasting Engineer, and Computer Network Administrator.

question: What jobs can Automation And Supply Chain Engineering graduates get?
ground_truth_answer: Automation Engineering graduates can work in: Automation Engineering & Supply Chain operation, Business analytic and optimization, Digital manufacturing, and Programming and business development.

question: What careers are available for Biotechnology and Food Technology Master graduates?
ground_truth_answer: Biotechnology Master graduates can work as: Biotechnology Researcher, Food Technology Specialist, Bioprocess Engineer, Quality Control Manager, and Research Scientist.

question: What job prospects do MITE (Master of Science in IT Engineering) graduates have?
ground_truth_answer: MITE graduates can work as: Software Developer/Programmer, IT Consultant, Project Manager in IT, Network Specialist, and Wireless Engineer.

question: Where is the Faculty of Engineering RUPP located?
ground_truth_answer: The Faculty of Engineering is located at Room 103, STEM Building, Royal University of Phnom Penh (Campus 1), Russian Federation Blvd (110), Phnom Penh.

question: Which building is the STEM office of FE-RUPP in?
ground_truth_answer: The STEM office is in the STEM Building, Room 103, at Royal University of Phnom Penh Campus 1.

question: What is the full address of the Faculty of Engineering at RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: Room 103, STEM Building, Royal University of Phnom Penh (Campus 1), Russian Federation Blvd (110), Phnom Penh, Cambodia.

question: Which campus of RUPP houses the Faculty of Engineering?
ground_truth_answer: The Faculty of Engineering is located at RUPP Campus 1 (Royal University of Phnom Penh).

question: What road or boulevard is Royal University of Phnom Penh located on?
ground_truth_answer: Royal University of Phnom Penh is located on Russian Federation Blvd (110), Phnom Penh.

question: Which room number is the STEM office of FE-RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The STEM office is in Room 103 of the STEM Building at RUPP.

question: In which city is FE-RUPP located?
ground_truth_answer: FE-RUPP is located in Phnom Penh, Cambodia.

question: What is the email address of the Faculty of Engineering RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The email address of FE-RUPP is fe.Info@rupp.edu.kh

question: What is the phone number of FE-RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The phone number of FE-RUPP is 096 328 4995.

question: What is the vision of the Faculty of Engineering at RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The vision of FE-RUPP is: To pursue an excellence in teaching, research and community engagement in Engineering Education to address the challenges of ever-changing society and globalization.

question: Who is the Dean of the Faculty of Engineering at RUPP?
ground_truth_answer: The Dean of the Faculty of Engineering at RUPP is Dr. Nguonphan Pheakdey.

question: What soft skills does FE-RUPP teach and how?
ground_truth_answer: FE-RUPP teaches the following soft skills: Communication, Team Work, Problem Solving, Innovation and Creativity, and English. These are taught for 4 hours per week over 3 years, with a Certification of completion awarded.
